member.office_address: 2365 Rayburn House Office Building Washington DC 20515-0532

form_analysis.form_header_text: Congressman Brad Sherman (CA-32) - Programmatic and Language Requests for Fiscal Year 2027 Appropriations
form_analysis.form_instructions_text:
Please use the below form to submit a Programmatic, Bill Language, or Report Language Appropriations Request for Fiscal Year 2027 (FY27). Please submit this form separately for each individual request you have. 

Note that this form is NOT to be used for Community Project Funding requests.

fields.label: Address (Street Address, City, State, & Zip Code)
Organization Address (Optional - Street Address, City, State, & Zip Code)
Choose an Appropriations Subcommittee
options: Agriculture, Rural Development, Food & Drug Administration
fields.rationale: NASA's Science Mission Directorate is funded under the Commerce, Justice, Science, and Related Agencies Subcommittee.

fields.label: Request Type
options: Programmatic (Funding) Request with Bill and/or Report Language
fields.rationale: The request context specifies this is a programmatic funding request for the Science Mission Directorate, with no report or bill language requested.

fields.label: Program and/or Language Title (for Defense requests, include PE number)
fields.rationale: Directly uses the short title provided in the reference values for the Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: Federal Agency
fields.rationale: NASA is the specified federal agency for this request.

fields.label: Agency Subdivision (if applicable)
fields.rationale: Science Mission Directorate (SMD) is the primary subdivision for this funding request.

fields.label: Account Name
fields.rationale: 'Science' is the official account name within the NASA budget structure.

fields.label: Bill/Report Section Title(s) (for language requests, if not applicable put N/A)
fields.rationale: This is a programmatic funding request, and the context indicates N/A for language requests.

fields.label: Select Funding Request Type
options: Support the President's FY27 budget request
Maximum support possible for the program
fields.rationale: The requested amount of $9,000,000,000 is a significant increase (24%) over the FY2026 enacted level of $7,250,000,000.

fields.label: Amount of funding requested (if not applicable, put N/A)
fields.rationale: This reflects the specific funding ask of $9 billion for NASA's Science Mission Directorate.

fields.label: President's FY2027 Budget Request (if not applicable, put N/A)
fields.rationale: The FY2027 President's Budget Request has not yet been released.

fields.label: Previously enacted funding amount from Fiscal Year 2026 (if not applicable, put N/A)
fields.rationale: This is the enacted total for FY2026 provided in the reference values.

fields.label: Provide requested language below.  Please indicate whether Bill/Report language or both.  (If not applicable, put N/A)
fields.rationale: The request context explicitly states that report and bill language are not applicable for this programmatic request.

fields.label: Previously enacted language from Fiscal Year 2026. Please indicate whether Bill/Report language or both. (If not applicable, put N/A)
fields.rationale: The provided context indicates N/A for requested language and does not provide specific historical text from H.R. 6938 beyond the dollar amount.

fields.label: Did you meet with Sherman's staff regarding this request?
fields.rationale: Information regarding specific constituent meetings with Representative Sherman's staff is not provided in the context.

fields.label: OPTIONAL: If answer above is "Yes" or "Future meeting has been scheduled" please indicate date of the meeting and name of Staff Member involved.
fields.rationale: This field requires user-specific meeting information not available in the provided context.

fields.label: List known organizations or number of individual persons benefitting in CA-32 (if applicable).
fields.draft_value:
* University of California, Los Angeles (UCLA): $61.2M in cumulative contracts and grants for lunar mapping and magnetosphere research.
* The Common Pool LLC (Santa Monica): $7.3M contract supporting crowdsourcing for mission problem-solving.
* Northrop Grumman Systems Corporation: $5.3M in contracts for technical aerospace development.
* Total NASA Science obligations in CA-32 across FY2023-FY2025 reached $19,398,886.38.
fields.rationale: Cites specific recipient names, award types (contracts/grants), and cumulative amounts directly from the NASA spending context for CA-32.

fields.label: If a House Member office (other than Sherman) is leading this request, please list below the name of the Member, the staff contact, and the email address of the staff contact. (Optional)
fields.rationale: This information is specific to the submitter's organizational coordination and is not provided in the context.

fields.label: Please include any additional notes or information below. (Optional)
fields.draft_value:
* Funding at $9B ensures US leadership in space science against rapid expansion by international competitors, particularly China.
* Every dollar invested in NASA returns approximately $3 to the economy, supporting CA-32's industrial and scientific workforce.
* This request restores stability following the FY2026 funding cuts, protecting decades of peer-reviewed scientific roadmaps.
* Space science is a fundamental public responsibility that advances national security and economic competitiveness.
fields.rationale: Incorporates strategic advocacy points regarding economic ROI, international competition, and the necessity of stable funding following FY2026.

top_contracts.description: Diviner Lunar Radiometer Experiment (DLRE), University of california-los Angeles (UCLA) segment, phase d/E effort under the LRO project
top_contracts.generated_award_summary: Funding UCLA's long-term mapping of the Moon's surface temperature.
Funding external crowdsourcing for complex mission problem-solving.

top_grants.description: A miniaturized  low power magnetometer system for lunar surface observatories
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Developing low-power magnetic sensors for lunar observatories

top_grants.description: 22-lws22_2-0027 foreshock transients' impact on magnetospheric perturbations and plasma population
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Investigating solar wind impacts on Earth's protective magnetosphere.

top_grants.description: X-rays from the inner galaxy'S exhaust vents
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Analyzing X-rays from the inner galaxy's energetic outflows.

top_grants.description: Time series xai model for improved landslide prediction
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Improving landslide prediction accuracy using time-based artificial intelligence.

top_grants.description: The spatial and energy distribution of precipitating relativistic electrons within the drift and bounce loss cones
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Tracking high-energy space radiation impacting Earth's protective shield.

top_grants.description: An automated framework for robust image proxies and data driven exploration of their relationship with total solar irradiance tsi and solar spectral irradiance ssi
top_grants.generated_award_summary: Developing automated analysis of solar energy's relationship with observational data.
